change_example: Red-flag threshold, triage ruleset, or safety preemption logic changes.
notes: This is the highest-risk class because it can change diversion, advice, and harm-preemption behavior directly.

change_example: Identity binding, callback-to-session seam, access grant, or authorization behavior changes.
notes: Identity and authorisation behavior can mis-bind the subject or reveal stale writable posture; it therefore requires privacy, security, and independent safety challenge.

change_example: Patient-visible status copy, trust indicators, writable posture, or same-shell continuity truth changes.
notes: This class is explicitly derived from the stale-writable and calm-truth invariants in the foundation protocol and forensic findings.

change_example: Booking confirmation, network ranking, pharmacy dispatch, or outcome truth-semantics changes.
notes: This class covers changes that could misstate whether actionability, dispatch, or outcome truth is actually safe to show or execute.

change_example: Release, runtime, publication, manifest, or route-binding changes that can alter safety posture.
notes: This class prevents route/runtime/publication drift from silently reopening stale mutable posture after approval.

change_example: External adapter, supplier, transport, or provider behavior changes that could alter safety posture.
notes: Supplier and adapter changes can move both safety and external onboarding posture, so the graph keeps NHS Login and IM1/SCAL deltas explicit.

change_example: Communications, callback, contact-route, or reachability behavior changes.
notes: Communications changes are not allowed to drift into a purely cosmetic class because callback failure can still become a safety problem.

change_example: Assistive capability, visible-slice, or human-approval policy changes.
notes: Phase 8 explicitly requires no-self-approval and independent safety signoff for assistive releases.

change_example: Copy-only change with no clinical meaning, no trust implication, and no writable-posture effect.
notes: This class stays explicitly narrow so it cannot swallow trust, identity, or patient-facing status changes by accident.

change_example: Infrastructure or refactor-only change with no patient-visible, trust, identity, supplier, or clinical effect.
notes: This is the conservative technical-only class derived under GAP_CHANGE_CLASS_DERIVATION_PURE_TECHNICAL_BOUNDARY_V1.
